The $20+ billion project — the largest public infrastructure project in Mexican history — was built partly through Mexico's army (Sedena) and faced fierce criticism from Maya communities and environmentalists for routing through jungle and archaeological zones. UNESCO demanded emergency heritage protection during construction.

sources.name: SICT — Tren Maya Official Reports
OHCHR — Communication on Tren Maya and indigenous consultation (2022)

events.title: Claudia Sheinbaum Elected by Historic 59.7% — Morena Sweeps Congress
events.detail: Claudia Sheinbaum (Morena) won the June 2, 2024 presidential election with 59.7% of the vote — the largest margin in Mexican democratic history. She defeated Xóchitl Gálvez (PAN/PRD/PRI coalition, 28.1%) and Jorge Álvarez Máynez (MC, 10.4%). Morena and allies also won supermajorities in both chambers of Congress, enabling constitutional amendments including the judicial reform. Sheinbaum, a climate scientist and former Mexico City mayor, was the first woman elected president in Mexico's 700-year history.

sources.name: INE — Cómputo final: elección presidencial 2024
Reuters — Sheinbaum wins Mexican election (June 2, 2024)

events.title: Claudia Sheinbaum Inaugurated — Mexico's First Female President
events.detail: Claudia Sheinbaum is inaugurated as Mexico's first female president, continuing AMLO's Morena movement but pledging a more security-forward approach. Her presidency inherits the Sinaloa civil war, the CJNG at its most powerful, and a Trump administration demanding action on fentanyl and cartel designations.

sources.name: Reuters
BBC

events.title: El Mencho (CJNG Leader) Killed in Tapalpa — Aided by US Intelligence
events.detail: Nemesio Oseguera Cervantes 'El Mencho,' founder and leader of CJNG — the most powerful Mexican drug cartel — is killed by the Mexican Army in a raid on his mountain hideout in Tapalpa, Jalisco. The operation was conducted with US intelligence support (DEA signals intelligence). El Mencho had been Mexico's most wanted criminal since El Chapo's arrest, with a $15 million US bounty. His death triggers immediate CJNG retaliation killing 25+ National Guard members and 14+ civilians, and opens a succession crisis with four commanders vying for control.

sources.name: SEDENA
Democracy Now
Al Jazeera
CNN

events.title: UN Rules Mexico Disappearances May Be Crimes Against Humanity
events.detail: The UN Committee on Enforced Disappearances (CED) requests the UN Secretary-General to urgently refer Mexico's disappearances crisis to the General Assembly — the first time such an escalation has been used. The Committee found 'well-founded indications' that enforced disappearances are being committed as crimes against humanity. At least 132,828 people are missing in Mexico's official registry. Mexico's government rejected the ruling as 'biased.'

sources.name: OHCHR
FIDH

events.title: El Mayo Sentencing Postponed Again — Now May 18; Defense Cites Mexico Violence
events.detail: The sentencing of Ismael 'El Mayo' Zambada García is postponed for the second time — from April 13, 2026 to May 18, 2026 — in Brooklyn federal court. Defense lawyer Frank Pérez cited ongoing violence and instability inside Mexico making it impossible to gather key case documentation. El Mayo, 77, faces mandatory life imprisonment plus a $15 billion forfeiture judgment — one of the largest in US drug trafficking history. The second postponement fuels speculation among legal observers that El Mayo may be providing ongoing cooperation to US prosecutors, with potential implications for the Chapitos' own pending US indictments.

sources.name: El Heraldo de Saltillo
US DOJ

events.title: Trump Designates Mexican Cartels as Foreign Terrorist Organizations
events.detail: President Trump signed an executive order in February 2025 designating eight Mexican drug trafficking organizations — including Sinaloa Cartel, CJNG, Los Zetas, and MS-13 — as Foreign Terrorist Organizations (FTOs). The designation triggered immediate bilateral crisis: Mexico's Foreign Ministry rejected it as a violation of sovereignty and a potential pretext for US military action on Mexican soil. The FTO designation enables prosecution of 'material support for terrorism,' complicates Mexico-US security cooperation, and has shaped subsequent USMCA renegotiation dynamics.

sources.name: US State Department — FTO Designation Notice (February 2025)
SRE — Comunicado de la Secretaría de Relaciones Exteriores (February 2025)

events.title: CIA-Chihuahua Diplomatic Row — US-Mexico Intelligence Friction at Border
events.detail: On April 19, 2026, a DEA/CIA-linked operation in Morelos, Chihuahua targeting fentanyl labs resulted in a convoy crash killing 2 US Embassy officers and 2 Mexican AEI state investigators. Governor Maru Campos of Chihuahua (PAN) had authorized the US intelligence presence without notifying the federal government. President Sheinbaum threatened federal sanctions against the governor and ordered the SRE to issue a formal diplomatic protest. US Ambassador Johnson acknowledged the incident but defended cross-border intelligence cooperation under FTO designation authorities.

sources.name: SRE — Comunicado diplomático (April 19, 2026)
El Universal — Sheinbaum vs. Chihuahua (April 20, 2026)
